briefing_role: Use for direct IOOS emergency-response stories from Hurricane Sandy.
key_metrics: More than 23000 cargo containers diverted before Sandy; 80 ships sent out of Hampton Roads at a cost of $10 million; historic comparison suggested up to $500 million in ship damage could otherwise have occurred.
evidence_profile: Strong operational case evidence from an official NOAA/NOS report.
attribution_profile: Strong IOOS attribution in staged evidence for event-specific decision support.
recommended_claim_language: NOAA reports that IOOS-supported information informed pre-storm vessel and cargo decisions during Sandy; use as operational benefit evidence and treat avoided-damage figures cautiously.
caveats: Single-event administrative case study; no full counterfactual dollar impact; source still needs page-level verification before final citation.

source_name: Estimating Economic Benefits from NOAA PORTS Information: A Case Study of Tampa Bay
briefing_role: Use for quantified PORTS benefits in port efficiency maritime safety and emergency response.
impact_domains: Port efficiency; Maritime safety; Coastal economies; Emergency response
key_metrics: $4.4-$7.0 million annual realized quantifiable benefit; $1.1 million potential phosphate-trade benefit; 1.3-3.2 deep-draft groundings per year potentially prevented; $200000-$900000 spill-response benefit; $500000 storm-surge response value.
evidence_profile: Local case-study valuation with several modeled or assumption-driven components.
attribution_profile: Strong to medium PORTS attribution; PORTS is NOAA observing infrastructure and staged evidence treats several rows as strong attribution.
recommended_claim_language: Case-study evidence suggests Tampa Bay PORTS produced measurable annual benefits, but quantified claims should be presented as local estimates and lower-bound or modeled values as appropriate.
caveats: Older case study; local conditions may not generalize; several components depend on expert judgment assumptions or hypothetical scenarios; verify tables and dollar years.

source_name: Estimating Economic Benefits from NOAA PORTS Information: A Case Study of Houston/Galveston
briefing_role: Use for the strongest port-safety and realized annual PORTS benefit example.
impact_domains: Port efficiency; Maritime safety; Coastal economies; Emergency response
key_metrics: $14.1-$15.6 million annual realized quantifiable benefit; about $10.5 million per year in avoided grounding costs; $1.0 million annual spill-response benefit; $500000 storm-surge response value; smaller draft and delay savings.
evidence_profile: Local case-study valuation combining observed relationships with modeled assumptions.
attribution_profile: Strong to medium PORTS attribution depending on row; safety and emergency rows are staged as strong attribution.
recommended_claim_language: Case-study evidence suggests Houston/Galveston PORTS generated measurable annual benefits, especially through grounding-risk reduction and emergency-response support.
caveats: Causality is not proven; some estimates use conservative attribution assumptions and dated damage models; verify table values and dollar years.

source_name: Estimating Economic Benefits from NOAA PORTS Information: A Case Study of the Columbia River
briefing_role: Use for Lower Columbia River navigation efficiency safety and response benefits.
impact_domains: Port efficiency; Maritime safety; Coastal economies; Emergency response
key_metrics: $7.4 million per year realized quantifiable benefit; $4.0 million cargo-efficiency value; $800000 delay-cost savings; $1.5 million avoided accident costs; $100000 spill-response benefit; $1.0 million flood-warning benefit.
evidence_profile: Local PORTS/LOADMAX case-study valuation with modeled components.
attribution_profile: Strong to medium PORTS attribution depending on row; LOADMAX/PORTS linkage is direct but some benefit pathways are assumption-sensitive.
recommended_claim_language: Case-study evidence suggests Columbia River PORTS/LOADMAX data supported cargo efficiency, safety, and response decisions with measurable annual benefits.
caveats: Some estimates are lower-confidence; hazardous-material and flood benefits depend heavily on assumed probabilities and contribution factors; verify tables before use.

source_name: The U.S. Integrated Ocean Observing System (IOOS): A Prototype User Valuation
briefing_role: Use for the aggregate user value of IOOS Regional Association data services.
key_metrics: $198.0 million annual total user value for data services at nine participating IOOS Regional Associations.
attribution_profile: Strong IOOS attribution for participating Regional Association data services.
recommended_claim_language: A prototype valuation estimated about $198 million per year in user value for data services from nine participating IOOS Regional Associations.
caveats: Prototype contingent-valuation study; not comprehensive for all IOOS benefits; limited to participating RAs and accessed data services; verify method and cited value before final use.

source_name: The Integrated Ocean Observing System High-Frequency Radar Network: Status and Local Regional and National Applications
briefing_role: Use for strong operational HF radar search-and-rescue evidence.
key_metrics: Search area reduced by about two-thirds after 96 hours when HF radar data were used in SAROPS.
evidence_profile: Strong operational demonstration evidence; not monetized.
attribution_profile: Strong IOOS attribution in staged evidence through the national HF radar network.
recommended_claim_language: Operational evidence indicates IOOS HF radar can substantially narrow maritime search areas in some cases.
caveats: Figure-based demonstration rather than broad monetized evaluation; may not generalize to all rescues or geographies; verify figure and context.

source_name: National IOOS HF Radar Search and Rescue Project
briefing_role: Use to support HF radar transition into USCG SAROPS and national SAR potential.
key_metrics: HF-radar-derived search area was three times smaller than HYCOM-derived search area in a simulated case; USCG estimate of 50 additional lives saved each year after national implementation.
evidence_profile: Modeled or demonstrated project evidence; not a direct economic valuation.
attribution_profile: Strong IOOS attribution in staged evidence for HF radar and SAROPS integration.
recommended_claim_language: IOOS-supported HF radar data improved SAROPS search-area performance in demonstrated cases and could improve national SAR outcomes under implementation assumptions.
caveats: Simulated and prospective evidence; no monetized life-saving estimate in this source; verify underlying Coast Guard estimate before using prominently.

source_name: Assessing the value of harmful algal bloom forecasts in the Pacific Northwest
briefing_role: Use as the best seafood/HAB quantified value-of-information example.
key_metrics: Modeled annual HAB forecast value around $1.6 million under plausible scenarios; modeled range $0.4-$3.0 million per year; operating cost comparison around $1.2 million.
attribution_profile: Strong to contextual attribution; values the Pacific Northwest HAB bulletin and observing/forecast system rather than IOOS alone.
recommended_claim_language: Modeled evidence indicates the Pacific Northwest HAB forecast system can generate positive annual economic value for shellfish-management decisions.
caveats: Scenario-dependent; depends on forecast accuracy event frequency and management response assumptions; verify access to article details before final citation.

briefing_role: Use for ocean technology sector scale and competitiveness context.
impact_domains: Ocean technology industry scale; Ocean technology workforce; Ocean technology revenue; International competitiveness; Sector growth; Business-base expansion; Market diversification
key_metrics: 814 U.S. Ocean Enterprise businesses; about 325000 employees; $8 billion revenue; 22 percent export revenue; 7 percent real-dollar growth from 2015 to 2020; almost 60 percent increase in identified businesses.
attribution_profile: Contextual attribution; measures broader ocean observing and information services rather than IOOS-caused revenue or jobs.
recommended_claim_language: NOAA/IOOS studies show that ocean observing and information services are a measurable U.S. technology cluster with significant revenue employment and export activity.
caveats: Do not present revenue employment or growth as caused by IOOS; sector definitions and survey methods affect comparability; verify latest report and definitions.

briefing_role: Use to frame the size of coastal and marine sectors affected by observing services.
key_metrics: 3.2 million employees; $369 billion in goods and services; $148 billion in wages in the U.S. marine economy.
attribution_profile: Contextual attribution only; not an IOOS impact estimate.
recommended_claim_language: NOAA data show the marine economy is large, which helps explain why coastal observations and decision support matter.
caveats: Use only for context and exposure; not IOOS-attributable benefit; excludes some employment categories; verify year and ENOW basis.

source_name: The Value of PORTS to the Nation
briefing_role: Use for national PORTS scenario framing and qualitative emergency-response examples.
impact_domains: Port efficiency; Maritime safety; Emergency response
key_metrics: $265.5 million potential annual port-efficiency value; $26.8 million annual commercial marine transportation safety value; $220.7 million ten-year NPV safety value; qualitative emergency-response examples in Baltimore and New York City.
evidence_profile: Modeled national scenario and brochure-level summary with qualitative examples.
attribution_profile: Strong to contextual PORTS attribution depending on row; not IOOS-wide by itself.
recommended_claim_language: NOAA estimated substantial potential national benefits from expanded PORTS coverage and documented emergency-response uses of PORTS data.
caveats: Use scenario language; method details may be summarized rather than fully documented; do not frame as current realized IOOS benefits.

source_name: The Potential Economic Benefits of Coastal Ocean Observing Systems: The Gulf of Maine
briefing_role: Use as a regional observing-system valuation example beyond PORTS.
impact_domains: Coastal economies; Emergency response; Maritime safety
key_metrics: More than $33 million annual potential benefits across selected sectors; $24 million annual potential SAR benefit; $0.75 million annual oil-spill mitigation benefit.
attribution_profile: Strong to contextual attribution for regional observing improvements; not observed realized IOOS impact.
recommended_claim_language: Preliminary analysis suggests selected Gulf of Maine observing-system benefits could exceed costs and provide meaningful SAR and spill-response value.
caveats: Exploratory and partial; authors call for more precise measurement; some categories are qualitative or uncertain; verify report version and assumptions.

source_name: The Potential Economic Benefits of Coastal Ocean Observing Systems: The Southeast Atlantic Region
briefing_role: Use as a second regional observing-system valuation if the briefing needs geographic breadth.
key_metrics: $170 million annual economic benefit of SEACOOS information under conservative assumptions.
attribution_profile: Strong attribution to SEACOOS information in staged evidence; older regional system context.
recommended_claim_language: The study estimated about $170 million per year in benefits from SEACOOS information under conservative assumptions; use as modeled evidence.
caveats: Older region-specific modeled estimate; not realized causal impact; verify access and methods before final citation.

briefing_role: Use for official shellfish/ocean-acidification service description and congressional-facing language.
key_metrics: Several thousand small farms nationwide harvest more than $600 million worth of sustainable shellfish; Pacific Coast farms and hatcheries could access real-time online OA data through IOOS; portal began operations in November 2014 with five shellfish hatchery or growing sites.
evidence_profile: Medium evidence for service scale and official program description.
attribution_profile: Strong IOOS attribution for OA data access and portal service; economic gains are not quantified.
recommended_claim_language: IOOS-provided ocean-acidification data access expanded adaptation capacity for shellfish farms and hatcheries, while direct competitiveness gains remain unquantified.
caveats: Use for official service description and sector scale, not measured profits or avoided losses; verify page and wording before final citation.

source_name: Colgan, Charles S. and Castelletto, Anthony. 2021/2023. The U.S. Integrated Ocean Observing System (IOOS): A Prototype User Valuation. Center for the Blue Economy, MIIS.
briefing_role: Use for the aggregate user value of IOOS Regional Association data services.
impact_domains: Aggregate user value of IOOS RA data
key_metrics: Total estimated annual user value: $198.0 million point estimate; 95% margin-of-error range $192.45-$207.52 million.
evidence_profile: Modeled contingent-valuation prototype study based on a survey of IOOS Regional Association website users.
attribution_profile: Strong IOOS attribution for data from 9 of the 11 IOOS Regional Association websites surveyed.
recommended_claim_language: A Center for the Blue Economy prototype study estimated the annual willingness-to-pay value of data from 9 of the 11 IOOS Regional Associations at about $198 million, with a roughly $192 million to $208 million 95% margin-of-error range.
caveats: Stated willingness-to-pay/user-surplus estimate, not measured avoided cost or realized economic benefit; two RAs did not participate; direct server users were excluded; authors describe the figure as likely an underestimate and explicitly prototype-stage.
